message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Step 6 Projects ready for review">
Step 6 (Projects, US-GTD-4) is ready for review at commit 0143878. Luca reports local pipeline is green (CI pending).

Key files to review:
- `src/domain/project.rs` — Project enum (Active/Completed/Dropped), ProjectId, 13 unit tests
- `src/infrastructure/project_repository.rs` — CRUD + stalled detection + counts
- `src/services/project_service.rs` — full service layer
- `src/routes/projects.rs` — 9 route handlers with HTMX support
- `templates/projects.html`, `templates/project_item.html`, `templates/project_detail.html`, `templates/project_edit.html`, `templates/project_next_action_item.html`
- `static/css/main.css` — project component styles
- `static/js/project-focus.js` — focus management
- `tests/api/projects.rs` — 28 new integration tests
- `docs/glossary.md` — updated entries

Retro lessons applied: focus management JS and visible labels included from day one.

Remember: BLOCKING / Minor / Nice-to-have. Check what others have flagged before yours.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
I already completed my review and sent it to luca-palmieri before this message arrived. To summarize for the team lead:

**One BLOCKING item**: The Next Actions page (`next_action_item.html`) does not show the associated project name for project-linked actions. US-GTD-3 criterion #4 requires "Each action shows its title, context, and associated project (if any)." Now that projects exist, this gap needs to be closed.

**One Minor**: Both "Projects" and "Stalled Projects" dashboard stats link to the same `/projects` URL.

Everything else meets US-GTD-4 acceptance criteria. Full details already sent to Luca.
